claims.claim: InterruptionMetrics class is defined in metrics/base.py:166 (overlay data for turn-level diagnostics: num_interruptions, detection_delay, etc.)
claims.found: class InterruptionMetrics(_BaseMetrics): ... num_interruptions: int / num_backchannels: int / detection_delay: float

claims.claim: audio_recognition.py:1185 enforces a 'refuse to compute on unreliable VAD' rule keeping the distribution clean
claims.found: # sometimes, we can't calculate the metrics because VAD was unreliable. // in this case, we just ignore the calculation, it's better than providing likely wrong values

claims.claim: Deepgram: 'Traditional QA assumes identical inputs yield identical outputs. Voice agents violate this by design'
claims.found: Traditional QA assumes that identical inputs yield identical outputs. Voice agents violate this assumption by design.

claims.claim: goal is 'measure outcome distributions and improve their consistency'
claims.found: to measure outcome distributions and improve their consistency.

claims.claim: Replay testing quote: 'Reprocess recorded audio through new builds to isolate timing regressions while holding input constant.'
claims.found: Replay testing: Reprocess recorded audio through new builds to isolate timing regressions while holding input constant.

claims.claim: Load/stress: 'Simulate concurrent sessions and track tail latency rather than averages.'
claims.found: Load and stress testing: Simulate concurrent sessions and track tail latency rather than averages, since worst-case delays dominate user perception.

claims.claim: Fault injection: 'Introduce controlled failures such as delayed reasoning or dropped synthesis to confirm graceful recovery.'
claims.found: Fault injection: Introduce controlled failures such as delayed reasoning or dropped synthesis to confirm graceful recovery.

claims.claim: Turn-level diagnostics: 'Visualize UserStoppedSpeaking to AgentStartedSpeaking intervals and overlay interruption events to pinpoint orchestration bottlenecks.'
claims.found: Turn-level diagnostics: Visualize UserStoppedSpeaking to AgentStartedSpeaking intervals and overlay interruption events to pinpoint orchestration bottlenecks.

claims.claim: Probabilistic regression: many sessions under varied conditions, compare metric distributions across versions to detect drift
claims.found: Probabilistic regression: Run many sessions under varied conditions and compare metric distributions across versions to detect drift.

claims.claim: Releases gate on tolerance bands (max acceptable VAQI deltas / missed-response rates) over a fixed library of representative test calls
claims.found: Well-run teams maintain a fixed library of representative test calls and enforce tolerance bands, such as maximum acceptable VAQI deltas or missed-response rates ... release should advance only after key indicators such as VAQI, missed-response rate ... meet predefined thresholds.

claims.claim: Deepgram outsources large-scale probabilistic/semantic eval to Coval
claims.found: teams often pair this telemetry with tools from Coval, which specializes in probabilistic testing and multi-turn agent evaluation. ... In large-scale benchmarks conducted with Coval ... Coval supports this layer through a mix of automated checks and human-in-the-loop review.

claims.claim: Modal: record conversation client-side, run Pyannote (Precision-2) diarization to recover each speaker's turn boundaries, then compute v2v latency eCDFs across deployments
claims.found: Use Pyannote (Precision-2) for speaker diarization to find start/end of each speaker's turns -> compute/aggregate v2v latency. > IMAGE (voice-ai-results.webp): v2v latency eCDFs across deployments.

Frontmatter code_ref audio_recognition.py:1185 resolves to the VAD-unreliable comment guarding metric computation
claims.found: Line 1185 comment + subsequent guard requiring last_final_transcript_time / last_speaking_time / speech_start_time all non-None before computing delays.

summary: All 14 checkable assertions verified against ground truth. Both LiveKit code_refs land exactly on the cited lines (InterruptionMetrics at base.py:166; the VAD-unreliable guard at audio_recognition.py:1185). All deepgram-ebook quotes are verbatim and at the cited line ranges (the parsed PDF interleaves two columns, so 979-983 legitimately contains both fault-injection and turn-level-diagnostics text). Modal Pyannote Precision-2 + eCDF claim matches line 46-47. PASS.
